title: A hose-on sprayer
abstract: A sprayer for attachment to a hose, the sprayer including an inlet for introducing fluid into the sprayer, an outlet for discharging fluid from the sprayer, a first controller to control the flow of fluid from the inlet to the outlet, a first fluid path located between the first controller and the outlet, a second fluid path located between the first controller and the outlet, the second fluid path fluidly connectable to a container having an active liquid, and a second controller to control the flow of fluid in the second fluid path, wherein in use, when fluid is flowing in the second fluid path, the fluid flow causes the active liquid to enter the second fluid path and exit the sprayer via the outlet.
claims: Claims 1 . A sprayer for attachment to a hose, the sprayer including an inlet for introducing fluid into the sprayer; an outlet for discharging fluid from the sprayer; a first controller to control the flow of fluid from the inlet to the outlet; a first fluid path located between the first controller and the outlet; a second fluid path located between the first controller and the outlet, the second fluid path fluidly connectable to a container having an active liquid; and a second controller to control the flow of fluid in the second fluid path, wherein in use, when fluid is flowing in the second fluid path, the fluid flow causes the active liquid to enter the second fluid path and exit the sprayer via the outlet. 2. A sprayer including an inlet for introducing fluid into the sprayer; an outlet for discharging fluid from the sprayer; a first controller to control the flow of fluid from the inlet to the outlet; a first fluid path located between the first controller and the outlet; a second fluid path located between the first controller and the outlet, the second fluid path fluidly connectable to a container having an active liquid; and a second controller to control the flow of fluid in the second fluid path, wherein in use, when fluid is flowing in the second fluid path, the fluid flow in the second fluid path causes the active liquid to exit the sprayer via the outlet. 3. A sprayer as claimed in claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the inlet includes a hose coupling attachable to a garden hose. 4. A sprayer as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the outlet includes a nozzle to discharge the fluid. 5. A sprayer as claimed in claim any one of the preceding claims, wherein the outlet includes a plurality of nozzles, and a desired nozzle is selectable from the plurality of nozzles. 6. A sprayer as claimed in claim 5, wherein the outlet includes a selector ring to select the desired nozzle from the plurality of nozzles. 7. A sprayer as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first controller includes a valve and a trigger, when actuated, the trigger opens the first controller valve. 8. A sprayer as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the second controller includes a valve and a switch, when actuated, the switch opens the second controller valve. 9. A sprayer as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, including the container. 10. A sprayer as claimed in claim 9, wherein the container is a rigid container. 1 1 . A sprayer as claimed in claim 9, wherein the container is a collapsible bladder. 12. A sprayer as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, including a flow controller to control the rate of flow of active liquid from the container. 13. A sprayer as claimed in claim 12, wherein the flow controller has a plurality of distinct flow settings. 14. A sprayer as claimed in claim 12 or claim 13, wherein the container operatively engages with the flow controller to control the flow of active liquid from the container. 15. A sprayer as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, including a flow indicator to indicate the flow of active liquid from the container. 18. A sprayer as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the second fluid path has a venturi arrangement, and wherein when fluid is flowing in the second fluid path, the fluid flow and the venturi arrangement causes a reduction in fluid pressure, drawing the active liquid from the container, the active liquid from the container is drawn into the fluid flow in the second fluid path and is discharged from the outlet. 17. A sprayer as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the fluid is water. 18. A method of spraying an active liquid using a sprayer as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, the method including the steps of introducing fluid into the sprayer via the inlet; actuating the first controller to allow at least a portion of the fluid to flow through the first fluid path; actuating the second controller to allow a portion of the fluid to flow through the second fluid path; and using the fluid flow in the second fluid path to move active liquid from the container and discharge the active liquid from the outlet. 19. A method as claimed in claim 18 further including the step of using a flow controller to control the rate of flow of active liquid from the container. 20. A method as claimed in claim 18 further including the step of indicating the flow of active liquid from the container using a flow indicator.
